reviews.review_text: Great caravan park..
The campsite has everything you need: a kitchen, restrooms, and a swimming pool. The lady at reception was lovely and gave us advice on what to do in Darwin. It's the cheapest campsite we found !

This is an OK budget caravan park, I stayed here because of proximity to motorcycle dealership for service. There is a fair amount of road noise & the ammenities are very basic & in desperate need of an upgrade but there is plenty of room in terms of campsites both powered & unpowered.
The layout is quite odd & there is a pool that is difficult to find but it’s got a great canopy of trees over it so lovely for relaxing, again, this needs to be maintained better and done up to date furniture added (the plastic loungers are broken).
The host is lovely & very helpful, I had an infestation of green ants the first night, advised the lady the next day & the area was sprayed, not sure I want to know what with but the ants disappeared quickly.
Overall not an unpleasant stay but could really do with some upgrades.

I would not recommend anyone staying at this Park. The Caretaker has a big problem with the true facts. He will kick you out all because he has a bee in his bonnet. He has kicked people out for no reason over nothing.
Absolutely Wonderful People to stay with, and close to everything. *****

Good 4 holiday need more onsite van showers nice pool nice quiet yes not dirt cheep though but very nice better and cheeper than motel room comfortable aircon aswell stayed thier 30 years ago was about 250 a week all inclusive pretty good price compared too other rentals
Great for location ang great staff let down by very old facilities out of a mine camp and the most expensive place we have stayed yet

Bad Cavan park ever, it doesn't have wi-fi or shade for your's pets or anything like that. It doesn't may tamed the pool properly because there always leafs in it (need a cover or something). The cleaner's walk in the toilet and start working without seeing if anyone in there and I'm only rating it 1 star because it's bit cheap and that it's near shops that you need~
Close to shops. A bit pricey tho. Nice pool.
Plenty of room, shady trees and friendly staff. Best in the NT.

Sometimes when you are a traveler it’s the people who run the place that make all the difference
When I was waiting to check in there was a bloke profusely thanking the woman behind the counter for being so wonderful and helpful
Over a week long stay I can 100% back him up.
Just beautiful people running a fantastic park.
It’s clean and well cared for.

Thanks so much guys you don’t know how much I appreciate your kindness
Pick your own site.  Got to like that.  Amenities might be old but they are really clean.  Can't ask more than that.

Good park position, was worried it was a bit far out of Darwin CBD but it was an easy drive in which we did daily for 5 days. The amenities are super dated and not good at all and the lights are on a very short timer so if using them at night be prepared to finish your shower in the dark unless another camper helps you out, which we all did. There are heaps of shops across the road like Coles and Kmart and also a big laundromat. The cinema is about 5 or so minutes up the road. Very convenient to everything.
Glad they could fit me in
Depends on what part of the park your staying in

BEWARE of staying at this caravan park. The caretakers are rude arrogant and they have no communication skills we asked a question to be told get out of the the park by the end of the week. The toilets block is old and you get eaten alive by mosquitoes while having a shower and they have no disability facilities. About 8 people have been kicked out in the last couple of months and a pensioner couple got kicked out when her husband was in hospital and she didn't know how to tow a caravan so she had to run around trying to get some help from someone.Definitely no compassion for anyone

Friendly staff
.good quiet location. Lots of good shady spots

Camped here for a week.
Amazing location, out of Darwin CBD, but has shopping centre walking distance.
Pricey, but that is Darwin in peak season.
Has a small dog park attached.
Plenty of people camped up, but always still felt quiet, even though it was reasonably busy
Friendly staff at check in

Well we stayed, for over 2 years and we Highly Don't Recommend this Park. Toilets have No proper Disabled facilities. Management are disgrace. They think it's okay to Drive ,10 to 20K around the park and tell people. [ I DON'T DRINK OR SMOKE].

On the outskirts as you head towards Darwin. An easy walk across to the shops and food venues. You just have to be careful crossing the highway. Next door to a servo.
The grounds are nothing fancy. Not very shady, but are neat and tidy.  The staff are very friendly and helpful.

Stayed 8 nights, nice quiet park, central to so much
Amenities well they are old, they are clean, plenty of hot water, water pressure near non existing when more than one stall used together, exposed plumbing means easy to contact hot water supply, really why do half a job guys very much  "Faulty Towers"
Forgot to take gate pin for after hours entry contacted via after hours number, and messaged via sms, thank God another resident came along as I didn't get a response till mid morning the following day
Over all a nice convenient park that with a little bit of tlc would have so much potential
Powered Site
Always stay here - older park but it's great for us - larger sites, walk to shops, quiet
Great staff, Great rates, Very peaceful and relaxing. Highly recommend
Great spot, clean and open areas. Lots of shade available and pet friendly.

We stayed for a week the park was shaded fantastic camp kitchen and amenities the location was walking distance to shops  security was good we felt safe would highly recommend  if travelling to see Darwin city or Crocodile attractions also wonderful hosts and make sure you say hi to Pooch 👋😁

Perfect mid way point to everything when visiting Darwin.
Location Location.
Shopping centres within walking distance (all majors,Woolworths,Coles,kmart etc)
Wonderful hosts.
Thanks Phil ,Anne Marie and Pooch ,we had a fantastic time.

Drei Nächte verbrachten wir hier mit unserem Camper an einer Stromseite (Pro Nacht 55$). Empfangsdame überaus freundlich. Viele Schattenplätze. Münzwechselautomat bei den Waschmaschinen (6$ pro Waschgang). Lebensmittelgeschäft, Bars, Restaurant fußläufig entfernt

My husband and I originally booked a powered site for 2 nights. We were pleasantly surprised at just how quiet and well maintained it is that we booked for an extra week. Good amenities, great pool and excellent staff. Close to everything we need.

We had a nice and quiet spot in the shade! In the back of the park next to the swimming pool with nice gold water which was good because it was warm. Facilities a bit out dated but very clean.
Nice and friendly staff.

We are staying in our van.
Management are awesome.
Always willing to help out.
Best park in town.

We had an excellent overnight stay here in a cabin, lots of room, the bed was super comfy and the shower had good pressure. Lots of options for different accommodation and camping and well maintained. Also pet friendly. Theres a tavern and a 24hr servo within short walking distance. Will definitely stay again.

This is a great little park. Yes its old but really clean and so well equipped in the kitchen and laundry. the pool was cool. Friendly staff. Close to all shops etc and that suited us. If your after a big 4 sort of place this is not for you. We had a cabin. We were only staying 1 night but ended up staying 4. We kept extending every morning.
De moeite om te doen
Loved this park. Close to local shops. Clean amenities. Rubbish bins collected often. Will be back

Have had to stay while partner was taken to hospital . The owners & staff have been really helpful .The sons bought me a meal they had cooked for me the night I arrived from queensland . It was so nice & reassuring to know they were concerned about my partner & myself . The whole family deserves to be really blessed for their hospitality & kindness & caring . I can't thank them enough for all their support.
Marie Boughton

Busy caravan park.
We like it??

The Coolalinga Caravan Park shower & toilet block is very 1970s era. There are signs in the showers saying to put the shower curtains inside the shower cubicle.
As most of the clientele is larger women who have trouble keeping balance in the very small cubicle, it's no wonder the shower curtains are pushed out side the cubicle and flood the floors.
Come on Coolalinga owners put effort into updating the shower cubicles, there are now bigger one available since the 1970s.
Lots of permanent residents.  Very small sites for RVs. Could not recommend.  Only lovely part of the experience was reception, she is wonderful.

Maybe not the most beautiful park to stay, but good enough. The facilities are clean and sufficient. The park is spacious and has a swimming pool.|It’s a bit outside of Darwin (20min.), but since its very affordable we prefer this location to the more expensive and entertaining parks.|Our kids had a great time exploring the park and one of the practical features is that the park is next to two shopping centres with a Coles and a Woolworths.||So, a good choice when you look for a caravan park near Darwin and the National Parks.
Idea location, we water are area to keep green. Friendly staff and amenities are cleaned daily, large camp kitchen.
The place wasn’t too bad, but we were placed at a site with neighbors on both sides, even though the area looked empty during the weekdays and didn't have any boundaries (trying to grow some trees).
Just passing through,  didn't stay there
Great place to stay.amenites are good.close to shopping.  Pools great bloody cold but refreshing.

Great place to stay so close to all the major shops. Fast food takeaway outlets have a tavern across the road, which is in walking distance.  He has some great places to go and see good fishing spots and 4wd tracks . The staff are very knowledgeable and friendly helpful nothing is too hard to sort out  great cool pool clean amenities, just a great place to stay
Old style park however very clean and friendly staff a peaceful spot and close to shops across the road

We had a group of friends stay here and recommend to us.|We have 2 dogs with us, and, great! Dog friendly on website, signage etc still today on 8th April 2024.|Office unattended around midday, rang number and lady came to meet and greet. No friendly welcome at all and told us no dogs allowed against everything we were led to believe.|Very disappointed but found great place dog friendly 3k North.||Probably ok to stay, but not for us, ever.
Great place to stay, short and long term! The pool was great and amenities always clean. They also got a kitchen area with microwave, little oven and gas cooker.
Great place,nice and quiet
great pk needs a lot of updating eg showers r old but very clean lovely n hot

I needed a site last minute and this was the only option. It's fine, it has the amenities you need and is close to shops. However the unpowered sites have absolutely 0 shade, so you need to spend all day outside if you're in a tent like me due to the extreme heat. The amenities desperately need to be updated too
A great location 25 mins south of Darwin CBD on Stuart Highway. Walk to big shops. Lovely cool shady pool. Several sites with big shady trees. Clean amenities.
cheap - ordinary - needs work
Very clean comfortable park, we are in a van so easy parking no trouble with sites, staff are excellent, amenities clean park well looked after great spot at a great price

Good prices for a place so central, although it lacks trees you can see they are trying to grow some currently. However the allocated camp sites can be quite small and not really well defined (both times we have came here we’ve had our neighbours not know where their site stops). The walk to the amenities were around 200-300m as they all were at the back of the premises.

The pool is well maintained, very shaded and they have plenty of washing/drying machines. The shower and toilet blocks have many cubicles but are not the nicest looking.
Bit far from Darwin CBD but otherwise it is the best value in Darwin. Try and get a site down the back under the huge trees. Otherwise the sites are exposed. Nice pool.

Nice clean roomy Caravan Park with friendly and helpful owners and staff. I would certainly recommend this park to all visitors and it is very handy to all shops as they are walking distance and it is only 20 minutes out of Darwin. We will definitely be back.
Nice Park not far from Darwin excellent shopping facilities nearby.
Good sized plots in the  caravan park and extra friendly reception. Good clean amenities

Went there with my brother and his family. Whilst we were there his dog died from suspected poison from the garden. They show no compassion or care. Then the owners parked in front of his accommodation and when he asked them why they are parking there they stated they the owners and do whatever they want to do. He said to f*** off and park elsewhere and the following day they kick him and his family out. He shouldn't said what he said but just lost his dog and they were so rude.

Informed them of reduction in stay - at least 1 pre pre arrival + on arrival + when leaving, so they could book out the space = my emails in junk even though used reply to sender + no refund for that 1 day less. No one in office to speak to during stay. No free internet.
